questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 desert = 沙漠
🔑 work = 工作
🔑 animal = 動物
🔑 world = 世界
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Long ago, my dear, the world was new and shining.
questions.explanationZh: 很久以前,親愛的聽眾,世界是嶄新而閃亮的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: All the animals began to learn to help Man.
questions.explanationZh: 所有動物都開始學著幫人類做事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel lived alone in the middle of the wide sand.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel live near other animals?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:獨自 + 沙漠中間 → 附近沒別人 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You see, my dear, the Camel did nothing all day long.
questions.explanationZh: 你看,親愛的,駱駝整天什麼也不做。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: When others asked, the Camel only made a short rude noise.
questions.question: How did the Camel feel about the questions?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:粗魯短聲 + 別人問就打發 → 他覺得被打擾 → 答 bothered。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He only said one thing: "Humph!" and turned away.
questions.explanationZh: 他只說一個字:「Humph!」然後就轉開。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It was his lazy way to push the others off.
questions.question: Why did the Camel use that word?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:懶 + 推開別人 → 他想自己安靜不被打擾 → 答 keep peace alone。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The other animals all worked hard from sun-up to sun-down.
questions.explanationZh: 其他動物從日出工作到日落。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The other animals slowly stopped coming to see him.
questions.question: How might the Camel feel each evening?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:大家漸漸不來 + 他總說 humph → 他變孤單疏離 → 答 quiet and apart。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And all he ever said was that one short word: "Humph!"
questions.explanationZh: 而他始終就只說這一個短短的字:「Humph!」……

storyBeat: 三個夥伴去找他 — 他們要告狀嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 horse = 馬
🔑 dog = 狗
🔑 ox = 牛
🔑 answer = 答話
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now you see, my dear, the Horse came first to the Camel.
questions.explanationZh: 現在你看,親愛的,馬第一個來找駱駝。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He said, "Come and trot with me like the rest of us."
questions.explanationZh: 他說:「跟我一起小跑,像我們其他人一樣。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel made the same short sound and turned his face away.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel agree to help?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:同樣的短聲 + 把臉轉開 → 拒絕 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then the Dog ran up with a bone in his teeth.
questions.explanationZh: 然後狗叼著一根骨頭跑過來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Dog wanted help to bring and pick up things for Man.
questions.question: What did the Dog ask the Camel to do?
questions.explanationZh: 帶東西回來 + 撿起來 → 幫忙搬。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Last of all, my dear, the Ox came slowly with his big head.
questions.explanationZh: 最後,親愛的,牛慢慢地用他的大頭走來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Each time, the Camel gave them the same short rude reply.
questions.question: How did the Camel answer the three?
questions.explanationZh: 每次都同樣粗魯 → 同樣粗魯的方式。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The three friends looked at each other for a long moment.
questions.explanationZh: 三個朋友互相看了很久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Three animals came one by one, and each one walked away with no help.
questions.question: What does this scene tell us about the team?
options: they will soon need help from someone else
the Camel is a great team leader
the desert will be empty by morning
optionsZh: 他們很快要找別人幫忙
questions.explanationZh: 推理:三人沒得到幫助 → 工作做不完 → 必須去找別人 → 答 need help from someone else。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then, without a word, the three turned and walked off to find Man...
questions.explanationZh: 然後三人一句話也沒說,轉身去找人類……

storyBeat: Man 訂規矩 — 沙塵裡誰來了?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 rule = 規矩
🔑 tired = 累
🔑 angry = 生氣
🔑 cloud = 雲
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You can guess, my dear, where the three went to tell their story.
questions.explanationZh: 你可以猜到,親愛的,三人去找誰說這件事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They told Man that the Camel would not lift a finger.
questions.explanationZh: 他們告訴人,駱駝連一根手指都不肯動。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man stood up at once and put both hands on his hips.
questions.questionEn: Was Man pleased with the news?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:馬上站起 + 雙手叉腰 → 嚴肅不高興 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Man said, "Then you three must work double every day."
questions.explanationZh: 人說:「那麼你們三個每天要做雙倍的工作。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They had to do their own work and the Camel's work too.
questions.question: How would their days now feel?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:自己的工作 + 駱駝的工作 → 時間不夠 → 答 longer and harder。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And so the three grew very tired and very, very angry.
questions.explanationZh: 於是三人變得非常累,而且非常非常生氣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their backs hurt and they kicked the sand each evening.
questions.question: How did the three feel after many days?
questions.explanationZh: 背痛 + 踢沙 → 痠痛又難過。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One evening, a tall pillar of dust came rolling across the sand.
questions.explanationZh: 一個晚上,一根高高的沙柱從沙地上滾過來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What came toward them across the desert?
questions.question: What came toward them?
questions.explanationZh: 滾來的沙柱 → 沙塵雲。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The dust rolled closer and closer, and inside it something moved...
questions.explanationZh: 沙塵越滾越近,裡面有東西在動……

storyBeat: Djinn 來了 — 他會幫誰?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 djinn = 精靈
🔑 story = 故事
🔑 ask = 問
🔑 help = 幫忙
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now, O dear listener, the dust opened and a Djinn stepped out.
questions.explanationZh: 親愛的聽眾,沙塵打開,一個精靈走了出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He looked at the three tired animals with kind eyes.
questions.explanationZh: 他用親切的眼睛看著三個疲累的動物。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down low so he was the same height as the Dog.
questions.questionEn: Did the Djinn act in a friendly way?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:坐低平視狗 → 友善 → 答 Yes

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Horse and Dog and Ox shared every part of their story.
questions.explanationZh: 馬、狗、牛把整件事都告訴他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They said one friend would not help and they did all his jobs.
questions.question: Why were they upset at the Camel?
questions.explanationZh: 不幫忙 + 別人做他的工 → 他逃避工作。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn rubbed his chin and thought for a long while.
questions.explanationZh: 精靈搓著下巴,想了很久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted to find out where this lazy friend was hiding.
questions.question: Why did the Djinn want to find that place?
options: speaking with him face to face
questions.explanationZh: 推理:聽完三人不平 + 想找懶夥伴 → 要去當面處理 → 答 talk face to face。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Where is this Camel?" the Djinn asked in a deep, soft voice.
questions.explanationZh: 「這隻駱駝在哪裡?」精靈用低沉柔軟的聲音問。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A new friend has come, has heard the three sad stories, and is ready to act.
questions.question: What is this scene mainly showing?
options: the Djinn meeting the three and getting ready
Man cooking dinner for the animals
optionsZh: 精靈見三人並準備行動
questions.explanationZh: 主旨 = 精靈見三人後準備出手。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will go to him myself," the Djinn said, and rose into the air...
questions.explanationZh: 「我自己去找他,」精靈說著,飛上了天空……

storyBeat: Djinn 警告 — 是什麼魔法?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 friend = 朋友
🔑 hear = 聽見
🔑 sit down = 坐下
🔑 magic = 魔法
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You see, my dear, the Djinn flew to the Howling Desert.
questions.explanationZh: 你看,親愛的,精靈飛到了 Howling Desert。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel sat under a thorn tree, doing nothing at all.
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝坐在一棵帶刺的樹下,什麼也沒做。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn watched him for many slow, quiet minutes.
questions.questionEn: Did the Djinn act in a rushed way?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:好幾分鐘 + 靜靜 → 不急 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn said, "My friend, I have heard a sad report."
questions.explanationZh: 精靈說:「我的朋友,我聽到一個難過的報告。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He spoke about a friend who would not lift his share.
questions.question: Who ended up paying for that choice?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:他不抬自己的份 → 別人要替他扛 → 答 the other animals。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel only said his one rude word once more: "Humph!"
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝再次只說他那個粗魯的字:「Humph!」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He chose to stick with the same lazy reply once again.
questions.question: How did the Camel choose to act?
questions.explanationZh: 又是那個粗魯字 → 同樣的粗魯回答。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn sat down and placed his chin upon his hand.
questions.explanationZh: 精靈坐下,把下巴擱在手上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What was the Djinn about to do?
questions.question: What was he about to do?
questions.explanationZh: 坐下 + 下巴在手 → 進入施法姿勢。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then, very slowly, the Djinn began to make a great Magic...
questions.explanationZh: 然後,很慢很慢地,精靈開始施一個大魔法……

storyBeat: 駝峰出現 — 背還會停嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 flat = 平的
🔑 back = 背
🔑 behind = 後面
🔑 hump = 駝峰
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now watch, my dear, what happened next under that hot sun.
questions.explanationZh: 現在仔細看,親愛的,在那烈日下發生了什麼。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel's back was flat and smooth, like a wide soft mat.
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝的背平平滑滑,像一塊寬寬的軟墊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: You could place a small cup on his back and it would not slide.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel already have a hump?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:杯子不滑 → 背平的 → 還沒駝峰 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel said "Humph!" one more time, just to be rude.
questions.explanationZh: 駱駝又說了一次「Humph!」,只是為了無禮。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And as he said that word, his flat back began to push up.
questions.question: What does this tell us about his rude word?
optionsZh: 一股改變他身體的力量
questions.explanationZh: 推理:一說 humph 背就變 → 那個字觸發了身體變化 → 答 changing his body。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It puffed up and puffed up and puffed up some more.
questions.explanationZh: 它鼓起來、又鼓起來、又再鼓起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: When he turned his head, a great soft shape was rising up behind.
questions.question: What did the Camel see behind him?
options: a new bump on his back
questions.explanationZh: 又大又軟的形狀在背後 → 背上新隆起。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: It was, my dear, his very own big hump — and it stayed.
questions.explanationZh: 親愛的,那是他自己的大駝峰 — 而且不會消失。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The soft shape stayed on him, and it did not go down again.
questions.question: What might this new shape mean for his future?
options: he can keep saying humph and stay free
he will carry something every single day
the other animals will give him gifts
Man will let him sleep all year
optionsZh: 他能繼續說 humph 保持自由
他每天都要帶著一個東西
questions.explanationZh: 推理:形狀留在身上不消失 → 他從此天天帶著它 → 答 carry something every day。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And the great hump rose higher and higher above his back...
questions.explanationZh: 巨大的駝峰在他背上越長越高、越長越高……

storyBeat: 教訓 — 你背上的是什麼?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 own = 自己
🔑 bring = 帶來
🔑 three days = 三天
🔑 forget = 忘記
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then the Djinn turned, my dear, and spoke clear and slow.
questions.explanationZh: 然後精靈轉過身,親愛的,慢慢清楚地說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He said, "That hump is what you brought upon yourself."
questions.explanationZh: 他說:「那個駝峰是你自己帶到自己身上的。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Camel hung his head and looked at the sand for a long time.
questions.questionEn: Did the Camel feel proud of his new hump?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:低頭看沙看很久 → 不是驕傲,是難過 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Djinn said, "Now you can work three days with no food."
questions.explanationZh: 精靈說:「現在你可以三天不吃東西也能工作。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big shape stored what he needed to live on the road.
questions.question: Why was that helpful for the Camel?
questions.explanationZh: 推理:身上有存糧 → 路上不必常停下 → 答 travel long without stopping。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And so, my dear, the Camel went off to find the other three.
questions.explanationZh: 於是,親愛的,駱駝去找另外三個夥伴。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: From that day, he carried bags across the sand with them.
questions.question: How did the Camel join the team at last?
questions.explanationZh: 搬袋子過沙 → 搬東西。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He still has that hump today, my dear, so he will never forget.
questions.explanationZh: 他至今還有那個駝峰,親愛的,所以他永遠不會忘。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The thing he tried to skip became the thing he carries every day.
questions.question: What does the hump teach us, my dear?
options: work you skip can come back heavier
all camels are born with humps
magic is the best way to fix things
optionsZh: 你逃的工作會變成包袱
questions.explanationZh: 推理:他逃的工作 → 變成背上的駝峰 → 逃 = 包袱。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And you, my dear — what hump might you be carrying tonight?
questions.explanationZh: 而你,親愛的 — 今晚你背上又是什麼樣的駝峰呢?
